mnemonics: R.I.T. - Runnable, Instantiate, Thread: Implement Runnable, Instantiate Thread object, call start()
keyPoints: A Thread can be created by extending the Thread class or implementing the Runnable interface.
The start() method is used to begin execution of the thread, which then calls the run() method.
Directly calling run() executes the code in the current thread, not a new one.
commonMistakes: Calling run() instead of start(), which doesn't create a new thread.
Not implementing the run() method, leading to no code execution for the thread.
